message:
feat(tests): add workspace failure replay tests and evolution plan framework

Introduce a comprehensive workspace failure replay test suite that exercises
each fixture against its declared command outcome contract. Fixtures now
specify per-surface expected outcomes (success, auto-recovery, doctor errors,
repair applied, etc.) instead of bare surface name lists, enabling
deterministic replay-style regression testing.

Key changes:
- New tests/workspace_failure_replay.rs: replay tests for all 7 workspace
  failure fixtures, covering startup/open, create, doctor, doctor --repair,
  sync --status, and fixture-specific surfaces
- WorkspaceEvolutionPlan/Step/Command framework in scenarios.rs for
  multi-step workspace evolution with snapshot/restore/mutation primitives
- Smoke-profile env handling in cli.rs (preserve BEADS_DIR, BR_OUTPUT_FORMAT,
  etc.) for non-hermetic tests against real workspace fixtures
- Non-hermetic smoke test in e2e_basic_lifecycle.rs verifying env-sensitive
  path routing through where/info/sync --status
- All 7 fixture.json files updated from expected_command_surface string
  arrays to structured expected_command_outcomes with typed outcomes
- dataset_registry.rs: WorkspaceFailureCommandOutcome enum and
  outcome_for() lookup on fixture metadata
- Issue tracker state updated

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -504,8 +504,8 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.3","title":"Define the incident bundle capture workflow for field failures","description":"## Purpose
Define the minimal reproducibility bundle we should gather the first time a field failure is reported so we stop losing crucial evidence.

## Bundle Contents
- failing command + stdout/stderr
- `br doctor --json`
- `metadata.json`
- `issues.jsonl`
- presence and hashes of `beads.db`, `beads.db-wal`, `beads.db-shm`, `beads.db-journal`
- environment overrides that affect discovery/path/output
- whether multiple agents/processes were active

## Acceptance Criteria
- The bundle is small enough to request quickly during incident response.
- The captured data is sufficient to classify the failure using the taxonomy from this track.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:25.615887382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T06:23:54.847666702Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T06:23:54.834341839Z","close_reason":"Defined the minimal incident evidence bundle for field failures in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["incident-response","ops","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:25.615887382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:55.594416915Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":191,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead exists because ad hoc bug reports waste time and collapse into speculation. If we cannot preserve the evidence bundle, we cannot expect to reproduce or eliminate field failures systematically.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","title":"Build a sanitized real-world workspace failure corpus and replay harness","description":"## Why
Hermetic tempdir tests are not enough. We need a standing corpus of realistic damaged or drifted workspaces and a way to replay them automatically.

## Scope
Create sanitized fixtures that model the important bad states and wire them into a replay harness that exercises open/read/write/doctor/sync behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The corpus includes the representative classes from the taxonomy.
- The replay harness can be run locally and in CI.
- Failures in the corpus are easy to inspect and update intentionally.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:27.047082628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:41.863684637Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fixtures","reliability","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:27.047082628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:41.863085696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":192,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This track converts anecdotal production pain into durable regression assets. The goal is not synthetic benchmark data; it is realistic workspace states that future code must handle safely.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","title":"Create a sanitized fixture corpus for representative corrupt and drifted workspaces","description":"## Purpose
Build a curated, sanitized fixture corpus for representative workspace failure states.

## Required Fixture Families
- corrupt or partially corrupt `beads.db`
- `beads.db` + stale/missing `-wal`/`-shm` combinations
- malformed schema / duplicate schema-entry states
- JSONL with malformed lines or conflict markers
- DB/JSONL/metadata disagreement
- path/env discovery edge cases that only appear outside hermetic tests
- interrupted rebuild/sync/export leftovers

## Acceptance Criteria
- Fixtures are sanitized and safe to store in-repo.
- Each fixture includes metadata explaining the intended state and expected classification.
- The corpus is organized so future incidents can add new fixtures instead of spawning one-off repro scripts.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:29.035540341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T12:08:20.419604596Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T12:08:20.414239862Z","close_reason":"Added sanitized workspace-failure fixture corpus and loader support","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["corruption","fixtures","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:04.515270731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:29.035540341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":193,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Key consideration: a fixture corpus only helps if it preserves the interesting structure of the failure. Do not over-normalize fixtures into clean states that no longer reproduce the real anomaly.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:30Z"},{"id":220,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claiming this now. Initial focus: inventory existing malformed/corrupt workspace fixtures and repro directories already in-repo, identify the canonical fixture layout under tests/fixtures or adjacent harness code, then add a sanitized metadata-backed corpus that downstream reliability beads can consume.","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:33:27Z"},{"id":222,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed fixture corpus setup under tests/fixtures/workspace_failures/. Added seven small, metadata-backed workspace cases covering corrupt DB text, JSONL conflict markers, DB/JSONL drift, duplicate config rows, metadata-driven custom paths, WAL-without-SHM sidecars, and interrupted rebuild leftovers. Added loader/support APIs in tests/common/dataset_registry.rs so tests can enumerate fixtures and materialize a transport-safe visible beads/ payload back into runtime .beads/ workspaces. Verification: cargo fmt --check; rch exec -- cargo test workspace_failure_fixture -- --nocapture; rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets; rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.","created_at":"2026-03-12T12:07:33Z"}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","title":"Add a replay harness that asserts command behavior against the fixture corpus","description":"## Purpose
Add a harness that mounts each fixture workspace and asserts the expected behavior across the main operational surfaces.

## Required Surfaces
- startup/open
- read commands (`list`, `show`, `ready`, `where`, `info`)
- write commands (`create`, `update`, `close`, `reopen`, `label`, `comments`, `delete` as appropriate)
- `doctor`
- `sync --status`, import/export/rebuild behavior where applicable

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each fixture records expected classification and expected command outcomes.
- The harness distinguishes: healthy, degraded-but-usable, recoverable, and unsafe.
- Regressions are reported in a way that makes the failing fixture/state obvious.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:30.673427289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T15:26:47.897918872Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["reliability","replay","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:22.349991073Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:30.673427289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:13.418006391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":194,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This is the bridge between plan-space and code-space. Once this bead lands, future corruption fixes should start by adding or updating a fixture expectation instead of inventing bespoke local repro steps.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:31Z"}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","title":"Generate deterministic long-lived workspace evolution scenarios","description":"## Purpose
Create long-lived workspace scenarios that accumulate real history over time rather than relying only on one-shot fresh temp workspaces.

## Cover
- many commands over one workspace
- restarts between commands
- mixed auto-import/auto-flush behavior
- repeated derived-state rebuilds
- state after partial failures and subsequent recovery attempts

## Acceptance Criteria
- Scenario generator can produce deterministic seeds for reproducible failures.
- Generated workspaces can be fed back into the fixture/replay system.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:32.337089325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:40.460091148Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["long-lived","reliability","state-drift"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:31.233437625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:32.337089325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:40.459575482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":195,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Rationale: production users do not reinitialize  for every command. Long-lived state accumulation is where 'suite is green but field is broken' bugs are born.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:33Z"}]}

Cargo verification via rch is currently blocked by a stale worker-side /data/projects/frankensqlite checkout (fsqlite-core remote_effects.rs still calling bulkhead.cancel_entry(entry_id) without the new Time arg), so leaving this bead in_progress until offloaded cargo passes cleanly.","created_at":"2026-03-12T15:52:03Z"}]}
+{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","title":"Generate deterministic long-lived workspace evolution scenarios","description":"## Purpose
Create long-lived workspace scenarios that accumulate real history over time rather than relying only on one-shot fresh temp workspaces.

## Cover
- many commands over one workspace
- restarts between commands
- mixed auto-import/auto-flush behavior
- repeated derived-state rebuilds
- state after partial failures and subsequent recovery attempts

## Acceptance Criteria
- Scenario generator can produce deterministic seeds for reproducible failures.
- Generated workspaces can be fed back into the fixture/replay system.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:32.337089325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T16:00:01.697780433Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T15:59:50.012415288Z","close_reason":"Implemented deterministic long-lived workspace evolution generator and replay handoff","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["long-lived","reliability","state-drift"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:31.233437625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:32.337089325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:40.459575482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":195,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Rationale: production users do not reinitialize  for every command. Long-lived state accumulation is where 'suite is green but field is broken' bugs are born.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:33Z"},{"id":224,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Starting now. Plan: add a deterministic long-lived workspace evolution generator in tests/common that can replay many-command histories across restarts, mixed auto-import/auto-flush modes, derived-state rebuilds, and recovery-after-failure paths. I’ll keep the output reusable so generated states can feed back into the fixture/replay corpus.","created_at":"2026-03-12T15:29:36Z"},{"id":227,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented a seeded long-lived workspace evolution layer in tests/common/scenarios.rs. It now supports deterministic multi-command workspace scripts with issue-ID capture, stale/no-auto-import flows, explicit flush/import cycles, JSONL corruption + snapshot restore, and materialization back into DatasetOverride/replay-style inputs. Verification: cargo fmt --check passed; isolated-target cargo check --all-targets passed; workspace_evolution test filter passed locally across the integration binaries that include tests/common. Repo-wide isolated-target clippy now fails only in tests/workspace_failure_replay.rs (parallel bead surface), not in this implementation.","created_at":"2026-03-12T16:00:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","title":"Expand crash, concurrency, and filesystem realism coverage","description":"## Why
The current concurrency coverage proves some lock semantics, but it does not yet model real agent swarms, crash timing, filesystem failures, or prolonged mixed usage on one workspace.

## Scope
Expand destructive realism: crash injection, multi-process contention, filesystem anomalies, and long-running stress.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reliability tests model the kinds of failures users actually trigger accidentally.
- We can answer "what happens if the process dies here?" for the critical write/sync phases.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:34.087986025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:50.935545188Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["chaos","concurrency","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:34.087986025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:50.934904899Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":196,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Track intent: move from 'SQLite locking works under a narrow benchmark' to 'the workspace survives hostile real-world timing and storage conditions.'","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","title":"Add a crash-injection matrix for all critical mutation and sync phases","description":"## Purpose
Add crash-injection coverage across the critical mutation and sync phases.

## Required Operations
- create
- update
- close/reopen/defer/undefer
- delete
- label/comment mutation
- sync import/export/rebuild/recovery paths

## Required Crash Points
- before primary write
- after primary write but before derived-state rebuild
- during temp-file write
- before/after rename
- before dirty-flag clear
- during/after backup/quarantine creation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each crash point has explicit expected post-restart behavior.
- No crash leaves the workspace silently misclassified as healthy.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:35.916711828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:58.412355354Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crash-consistency","reliability","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:49.444077784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:58.411766251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:35.916711828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":197,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Future-self note: this bead is about interruption semantics, not throughput. Prefer fewer operations with well-defined crash points over broad but shallow coverage.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","title":"Broaden multi-process and mixed-agent contention scenarios","description":"## Purpose
Expand concurrency coverage from narrow lock-timeout scenarios to realistic multi-process and mixed-agent usage.

## Cover
- concurrent writers on one workspace
- read/write races across commands
- multiple agents issuing different command families interleaved
- routed/external workspace access during local mutation
- background processes touching the same `.beads` directory

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests define which outcomes are acceptable vs unacceptable.
- Contention results in bounded failure/retry behavior, not drift or corruption.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:37.773727464Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T11:38:47.309252200Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T11:31:29.228335520Z","close_reason":"Expanded bounded-failure concurrency coverage and validated integrity invariants","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agents","concurrency","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:09.543615058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:37.773727464Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":198,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Important consideration: success here does not mean every race magically succeeds. It means failures are explicit, bounded, and non-corrupting.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:39Z"},{"id":218,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Working this now in tests/e2e_concurrency.rs. Scope: add coverage for interleaved command families on one workspace, plus external BEADS_DIR/status access against the same workspace during local mutation, with final integrity assertions to distinguish acceptable lock contention from corruption/drift.","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:07:24Z"},{"id":219,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed in tests/e2e_concurrency.rs. Added env-clearing/env-aware helper coverage, broadened contention assertions to bounded explicit failures vs corruption, added interleaved-command-family and external-BEADS_DIR/background-status scenarios, and tightened existing concurrency cases to assert final integrity based on successful operations. Verification: rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_concurrency -- --nocapture, rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets, rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check.","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:31:44Z"},{"id":221,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added broader contention coverage in tests/e2e_concurrency.rs for mixed command families, routed/external access, background sync status checks, and actor-oriented mutation scenarios. Also stabilized e2e_concurrent_writes_succeed_with_retry with an explicit lock timeout for deterministic remote verification. Verified with rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_concurrency -- --nocapture, rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_concurrency e2e_concurrent_writes_succeed_with_retry -- --nocapture, rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets, and cargo fmt --check.","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:38:47Z"}]}
@@ -521,7 +521,7 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","title":"Make real-world reliability suites mandatory in CI and release verification","description":"## Purpose
Promote the real-world reliability suites from optional or ad hoc checks into mandatory gates for CI, release verification, and high-risk changes.

## Cover
- failure-corpus replay
- crash-injection suites
- long-lived-workspace stress
- doctor/recovery postcondition checks

## Acceptance Criteria
- shipping without these suites requires an explicit conscious override, not omission by default

 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.2","title":"Add structured observability and audit records for anomaly and recovery paths","description":"## Purpose
Emit structured logs, audit records, and counters for anomaly classification, quarantine, recovery attempts, and post-recovery validation outcomes.

## Scope
- record what evidence was seen
- record which automatic path ran
- record whether the outcome was success, refusal, or degraded mode
- make the resulting data easy to correlate with operator reports and bug fixtures

## Acceptance Criteria
- field incidents can be mapped back to concrete anomaly and recovery states
- debugging no longer depends on sparse ad hoc stderr snippets","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:00.957711972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:40.216518351Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","observability","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:41.051238985Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:00.957711972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":209,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"A system that recovers or refuses without leaving a clear audit trail is still opaque. Observability is what turns one scary field report into reproducible engineering input.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.3","title":"Rewrite troubleshooting and disaster-recovery playbooks around the hardened model","description":"## Purpose
Rewrite troubleshooting and disaster-recovery docs so they match the hardened product model rather than the current optimistic or partially outdated assumptions.

## Scope
- explain anomaly classes and what they mean
- document quarantine and evidence-preservation behavior
- provide step-by-step operator flows for safe diagnosis, escalation, and recovery
- make it clear when JSONL-only workflows are safe and when they are emergency fallbacks

## Acceptance Criteria
- future operators do not have to rediscover recovery strategy from source code or chat history
- docs match the actual behavior of `doctor`, startup, sync, and repair paths","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:04.382375983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:43.282354620Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","operations","recovery"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:50.583791301Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:59.792267448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:04.382375983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:58:09.055748700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":210,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead is about preserving hard-won reliability knowledge.  {"id":"beads_rust-rt7z","title":"Integrate rich output into quick commands (q, reopen, defer, undefer, count)","description":"## Commands: br q, br reopen, br defer, br undefer, br count

### Traffic Level: LOW-MEDIUM
Quick action commands that need simple confirmations.

### br q (quick create)
```
✓ Created beads_rust-abc1
  "Quick note about something"
```

### br reopen <ID>
```
✓ Reopened beads_rust-abc1
  Status: closed → open
```

### br defer <ID> [UNTIL]
```
✓ Deferred beads_rust-abc1
  Status: open → deferred
  Until: 2024-02-01 (7 days)
```

### br undefer <ID>
```
✓ Undeferred beads_rust-abc1
  Status: deferred → open
```

### br count [--status STATUS]
```
╭─ Issue Counts ──────────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                     │
│  Total: 156                                         │
│  Open: 89    In Progress: 34    Closed: 33          │
│  Ready: 23   Blocked: 12   Deferred: 5              │
│                                                     │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

---

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
Location: tests/cli/quick_tests.rs

```rust
#[test]
fn test_q_creates_issue() {
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let output = render_q_result("Quick note", &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("Created"));
}

#[test]
fn test_reopen_shows_status_change() {
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let output = render_reopen_result("test-id", &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("closed"));
    assert!(output.contains("open"));
}

#[test]
fn test_defer_shows_until_date() {
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let until = chrono::Utc::now() + chrono::Duration::days(7);
    let output = render_defer_result("test-id", until, &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("deferred") || output.contains("Until"));
}

#[test]
fn test_count_json_unchanged() {
    let current = run_count_json();
    let baseline = load_fixture("tests/fixtures/json_baseline/count.json");
    assert_json_eq!(current, baseline);
}
```

### E2E Test Script
Location: tests/e2e/quick_e2e.sh

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
source "$(dirname "$0")/../common/test_helpers.sh"

log_info "=== E2E: Quick Commands ==="
setup_test_db

# Test q (quick create)
log_step "Testing br q"
Q_OUTPUT=$(br q "Quick task" 2>&1)
assert_contains "$Q_OUTPUT" "Created"
Q_ID=$(echo "$Q_OUTPUT" | grep -oE 'beads_rust-[a-z0-9]+' | head -1)
log_pass "br q works"

# Test count
log_step "Testing br count"
COUNT_OUTPUT=$(br count 2>&1)
assert_contains "$COUNT_OUTPUT" "Total"
log_pass "br count works"

# Test defer
log_step "Testing br defer"
DEFER_OUTPUT=$(br defer "$Q_ID" "2099-01-01" 2>&1)
assert_contains "$DEFER_OUTPUT" "deferred"
log_pass "br defer works"

# Test undefer
log_step "Testing br undefer"
UNDEFER_OUTPUT=$(br undefer "$Q_ID" 2>&1)
assert_contains "$UNDEFER_OUTPUT" "open"
log_pass "br undefer works"

# Close and test reopen
br close "$Q_ID" 2>&1 > /dev/null
log_step "Testing br reopen"
REOPEN_OUTPUT=$(br reopen "$Q_ID" 2>&1)
assert_contains "$REOPEN_OUTPUT" "Reopened"
log_pass "br reopen works"

log_success "=== Quick commands E2E: ALL PASSED ==="
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log quick command type and result
- Log status changes for reopen/defer/undefer
- Log rendering mode","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:37:16.000283475Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T20:00:19.949044682Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T20:00:19.948988286Z","close_reason":"Rich output integrated for q, reopen, defer, undefer, count commands. Because CLI overrides merge last, that prevents environment or project startup config from enabling those modes unless the user passes the flag explicitly. Fix CLI override construction so absent startup bool flags stay None, and add regression coverage.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-12T01:51:36.080215737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T01:51:41.088573366Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rxg","title":"Config system (YAML + DB precedence + metadata.json)","description":"# Config System (YAML + DB + metadata.json)

## Purpose
Implement classic bd configuration semantics: **YAML config files + env vars + DB config table**, plus `.beads/metadata.json` for startup file paths. This is foundational for correct prefix handling, auto-import/flush flags, and routing behavior.

## Sources & Precedence (highest wins)
1. **CLI flags**
2. **Environment variables** (`BD_*`, plus select `BEADS_*`)
3. **Project config** `.beads/config.yaml` (walk up from CWD)
4. **User config** `~/.config/bd/config.yaml`
5. **Legacy user config** `~/.beads/config.yaml`
6. **DB config table** (runtime keys)
7. **Defaults**

## YAML-only Keys (startup settings)
Must live in config.yaml (cannot be stored in DB):
- `no-db`, `no-daemon`, `no-auto-flush`, `no-auto-import`, `json`
- `db`, `actor`, `identity`
- `flush-debounce`, `lock-timeout`, `remote-sync-interval`
- `git.*`, `no-git-ops`, `no-push` (read-only in br)
- `sync-branch` / `sync.branch` (ignored in br v1)
- `routing.*`, `directory.labels`, `external_projects`, `validation.*`, `hierarchy.max-depth`

## DB Config Keys (classic)
- `issue_prefix`
- `allowed_prefixes`
- `status.custom`, `types.custom`
- `import.missing_parents`
- `export.error_policy`, `auto_export.error_policy`, `export.retry_attempts`, `export.retry_backoff_ms`, `export.skip_encoding_errors`, `export.write_manifest`
- `max_collision_prob`, `min_hash_length`, `max_hash_length`

## metadata.json (startup file config)
File: `.beads/metadata.json`
Fields:
- `database` (DB filename, default `beads.db`)
- `jsonl_export` (JSONL filename, default `issues.jsonl`)
- `backend` (ignore dolt in br)
- `deletions_retention_days` (legacy, ignore)

If missing, use defaults. Legacy `config.json` should be auto-migrated to `metadata.json` on init (optional).

## Env Bindings
- `BD_*` (dots/hyphens => underscores)
- Legacy: `BEADS_FLUSH_DEBOUNCE`, `BEADS_AUTO_START_DAEMON`, `BEADS_IDENTITY`, `BEADS_REMOTE_SYNC_INTERVAL`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Correct precedence order for all config sources.
- YAML-only keys never stored in DB.
- metadata.json controls DB/JSONL paths before DB open.
- Env overrides honored.

## Tests
- Precedence tests (env overrides YAML overrides DB).
- metadata.json path override tests.
- YAML-only key handling tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:17:13.657995776Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T17:26:32.599293076Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T17:26:32.599293076Z","close_reason":"Implemented config precedence, startup resolution, metadata path handling, lock-timeout support, and tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
